title: fraxinus caudata
intro: Fraxinus caudata, also known as the Arizona ash, is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Fraxinus caudata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. The herbal preparations of Fraxinus caudata typically involve infusing the bark or leaves in hot water to create a tea, or using the resin to make a topical ointment.
